title: Caramelized Croatian Millet Pilaf
description: A comforting, low-sodium Croatian pilaf where millet is caramelized with onions and peppers for a savory, dense flavor perfect for a lazy Sunday.
instructions: Rinse the millet under cold running water until the water runs clear, then drain.
Heat the low-sodium vegetable broth in a medium saucepan until it is just simmering.

In a large skillet, melt the butter with the olive oil over medium heat. Add the diced onion, carrot, and bell pepper, sautéing until the vegetables soften and begin to brown, about 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the smoked paprika, ensuring the vegetables are evenly coated with the spice.
Add the rinsed millet to the skillet, stirring to combine. Let the millet toast for 2-3 minutes, uncapped, until it turns a light golden color and releases a nutty aroma.

Pour the simmering broth over the millet, bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the millet is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the skillet from the heat, fluff the pilaf with a fork, stir in the chopped parsley, season with a pinch of salt if desired, and serve hot.
